problem: helix-cli needs a GitHub Actions workflow to auto-publish to npm on push to main, a README.md, and CLI artifact retrieval commands. No CI/CD infrastructure exists. The user's continuation context emphasizes the publish workflow and setup instructions for GitHub/npm secrets.

files.why: Package metadata: name @projectxinnovation/helix-cli, version 1.2.0, bin hlx→dist/index.js, files: ['dist'], scripts build/typecheck only. No prepublishOnly or test script. dist/ is gitignored so it must be built during publish.
TypeScript config: target ES2022, module Node16, rootDir src/, outDir dist/, strict mode, declaration files generated.

CLI entry point with shebang. Routes commands: login, inspect, comments, --version. Line 47 hardcodes version '0.1.0' but package.json says '1.2.0' — version drift. New 'artifacts' command branch needed here.

Config loader: env vars HELIX_API_KEY/HELIX_INSPECT_TOKEN/HELIX_INSPECT_API_KEY + HELIX_URL/HELIX_INSPECT_BASE_URL/HELIX_INSPECT_URL. Fallback: ~/.hlx/config.json. Exports HxConfig type {apiKey, url}. Needed for README documentation.

HTTP client (hxFetch) with retry (3 attempts, exponential backoff, 30s timeout). Auth: hxi_ prefix → X-API-Key header, else Bearer. Default basePath /api/inspect, overridable per call. Artifact commands will reuse this.
Repository name/ID resolution helper. Lists repos via GET /api/inspect/repositories.
OAuth browser flow and manual API key entry. Saves to ~/.hlx/config.json.
Inspect command router (repos/db/logs/api). Established module pattern for new commands.
Lists inspection repos. Shows standard subcommand pattern: import config, call hxFetch, print output.
Database query command. POST request pattern with resolve-repo.
Log query command with optional --limit flag.
Comments command router (list/post). Uses basePath '/api'. Resolves ticket ID via --ticket flag or HELIX_TICKET_ID env var. Pattern reference for artifacts module.
Comments list: typed response, hxFetch with basePath '/api', client-side filtering, human-readable output.
Ignores node_modules/ and dist/. Confirms dist must be built during publish workflow.

facts: Package is @projectxinnovation/helix-cli, scoped under @projectxinnovation npm org
Two versions published on npm: 1.1.0 and 1.2.0. Latest is 1.2.0, published 2 weeks ago manually by usherpx@projectxinnovation.com
No .github/ directory exists — zero CI/CD workflows of any kind
No README.md exists at repository root
No test files exist (no .test.ts, .spec.ts, __tests__/); no test script in package.json
No .npmrc or .npmignore files exist
dist/ is gitignored and does not exist in repo. npm pack --dry-run without build produces only package.json (371 bytes). Published 1.2.0 is 14.5 kB — prior publishes included built dist/
package.json version is 1.2.0 but src/index.ts line 47 hardcodes '0.1.0' for --version output
package.json scripts: build (tsc), typecheck (tsc --noEmit). No test, lint, prepublishOnly, or prepack scripts
package.json files field restricts published content to ['dist'] directory only
package.json engines: node >=18. Package type is 'module' (ESM-only)
Package license is MIT
CLI bin entry: hlx → dist/index.js
Zero runtime dependencies — all Node.js builtins + global fetch
Auth sends hxi_ keys via X-API-Key header, other tokens via Authorization: Bearer header
Config resolution: env vars (HELIX_API_KEY + HELIX_URL) → config file (~/.hlx/config.json)

CORRECTION: Server artifact endpoints ARE accessible via inspection auth tokens. Direct reading of helix-global-server/src/routes/api.ts lines 236-240 shows: artifact routes at lines 237-238 are registered BEFORE requireAuth at line 240, with attachInspectionAuth + requireCommentAuth middleware
GET /api/tickets/:ticketId/artifacts?runId=X returns { items: [{ id, label, repoUrl, runId, branch, path, url }], stepArtifactSummary: [{ stepId, repoKey }] }
GET /api/tickets/:ticketId/runs/:runId/step-artifacts/:stepId?repoKey=X returns { stepId, repoKey, files: [{ name, content, contentType }] }
HELIX_TICKET_ID env var is already used by comments commands for ticket resolution — artifact commands should follow same pattern
Artifacts stored in Vercel Blob at paths like artifacts/{runId}/{repoKey}/{stepId}/{filename}
GitHub artifact URLs optionally built from committed branch artifacts (controlled by org.commitArtifactsToGithub)
No ORM or database in helix-cli — no migration awareness needed
unknowns: Whether the npm @projectxinnovation org requires 2FA for publishing and whether Automation tokens bypass it
Whether the package should remain public (current MIT license suggests yes) or restricted access
Whether team prefers publish-on-every-push-to-main vs publish-only-on-version-bump strategy
Whether npm access token should be Classic Automation type or Granular Access Token scoped to package
What the npm org's publish permission model is (which accounts have publish rights)
Whether version hardcode at src/index.ts:47 should be dynamically read from package.json at runtime or derived during build
Whether the ticket's reference to 'GitHub Actions artifacts' means the GitHub Actions Artifact API, or the Helix artifacts stored in Vercel Blob + GitHub branches
Whether Vercel-specific artifact storage exists separately from the blob storage served through the existing Helix server API
What format the OAuth login flow returns — session JWT or hxi_ API key — affects whether OAuth-obtained credentials work with artifact endpoints
Whether HELIX_RUN_ID env var should be supported alongside --run flag for artifact commands
Whether artifact retrieval should support --json output mode from the start
